metadata.title: States PCS: Assam GK Questions for Assam Public Service Commission Exams

questions.question: What is the popular nickname for the Assamese film industry?
questions.explanation: Assamese cinema is popularly called Jollywood. It produces films in the Assamese language and has been active since the 1930s, reflecting Assam’s culture and heritage.

questions.question: Which traditional Meitei symbol resembles pythons in Meitei cosmology and rituals?
questions.explanation: Paphal is a traditional Meitei symbol resembling pythons linked to Meitei cosmology and Sanamahism rituals. It represents life cycles and unity. Paphals are classified into tail-biting, non-tail-biting, and belly-crawling types. These symbols hold cultural and martial significance in Meitei tradition.

questions.question: Which trophy does Assam football team compete for in state-level tournaments?
questions.explanation: The Santosh Trophy is an annual Indian football tournament where state teams, including Assam, compete. The Durand Cup, IFA Shield, and Federation Cup are club-level competitions, not state-level tournaments. Assam’s representation in the Santosh Trophy began after the tournament’s inception in 1941. The tournament is organized by the All India Football Federation for states and government institutions.

questions.question: Which regional film industry was part of Filmfare Awards East until 2015-16?
questions.explanation: Filmfare Awards East started including Assamese films in 2014 alongside Bengali and Odia films. Assamese categories featured Best Film, Director, Actor, and Actress. The Assamese film category was discontinued after 2015-16. Afterward, the awards focused primarily on Bengali films until the event was rebranded as Filmfare Awards Bangla.

questions.question: Who founded the traditional Assamese art form Bhaona in the 16th century?
questions.explanation: Bhaona, a traditional Assamese art form, was established in the 16th century by Vaishnavite saint Sankardev. It includes religious plays called Ankiya Nat, featuring actors (Bhaoriya), singers (Gayan), instrumentalists (Baayan), and a narrator (sutradhar).

questions.question: Who did Sankardeva honor as ‘Opromadi Kobi’ for translating Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Madhava Kandali translated the Ramayana into Assamese, the first Indo-Aryan vernacular. Sankardeva honored him with the title ‘Opromadi Kobi’ for this translation.

questions.question: Who was the first martyr of Assam’s Medium Movement in 1972?
questions.explanation: Swahid Mozammil Haque, aged 12, was the first martyr of Assam’s Medium Movement in 1972. Several institutions, including Cotton University hostel and a government primary school, are named in his honor. Assam Sahitya Sabha and All Assam Goriya Yuva Parishad present awards commemorating his legacy.

questions.question: In which year was the Manipuri Department at Assam University, Silchar established?
questions.explanation: The Department of Manipuri at Assam University, Silchar, was established in 1997. It offers degrees including M.Sc., M.A., M.Phil., Ph.D., and D.Litt. focusing on Meitei language, culture, folklore, and literature. The department also supports affiliated colleges with syllabi emphasizing folklore studies.

questions.question: Who is the Bodo poet awarded the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2005?
questions.explanation: Mangal Singh Hazowary is a Bodo language poet from Assam. He received the Sahitya Akademi Award in 2005 for his work “Ziuni Mwkthang Bisombi Arw Aroj.” He was also honored with the Padma Shri for his contributions to literature and education.

questions.question: Under which schedule was the Naga Tribal Area placed after 1950?
questions.explanation: After 1950, the Naga Tribal Area was included in the Sixth Schedule (Part B) of the Indian Constitution. It was administered by the Governor of Assam as the President’s Agent. The Sixth Schedule provides for administration of tribal areas in northeast India to safeguard their autonomy and customary laws.
